markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Duo Dragon Kick](/Feats.aspx?ID=7075) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 110

**Prerequisites**
[construct innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=2)

**Requirements**
Your construct innovation is adjacent to you.

</column>

---

You’ve practiced an impressive spin technique with your construct, automatically attacking in tandem as you launch each other through the air. You Command your construct companion to fight alongside you, after which you both [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) in the same direction, making an unarmed [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) at the start or end of the Leap; this uses your companion’s actions for the turn. If the Strikes both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purposes of [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2893) and weaknesses.

**Unstable** Function You spin farther and higher, though the extreme movement risks discombobulating your innovation. Add the [unstable](/Traits.aspx?ID=416) trait to Duo Dragon Kick. Increase the distance of your Leaps by 10 feet if horizontal or 5 feet if vertical.
name: Duo Dragon Kick
requirement_markdown: Your construct innovation is adjacent to you.

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 110 **Prerequisites** [armor innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=1), [construct innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=2), or [weapon innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=3) **Access** Tian Xia origin --- Taking inspiration from [Desna’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=281) image as a giant silk moth in Tian Xia, you’ve attached a bracelet to your innovation that generates ultra-strong silk strands mixed with venom. You can unleash this substance as part of an attack. Make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). If it hits, the target takes damage from the Strike as normal and must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC with the following effects. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The silk imposes a –10 foot status penalty to all the target’s Speeds for 1 round.
**Failure** As success, and the target takes an additional 1d4 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The poison damage increases to 2d4 at 12th level and 3d4 at 18th level.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until the end of their next turn.
name: Silk Bracelet

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Xidao Sea Mine Drop](/Feats.aspx?ID=7077) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 110

**Prerequisites**
[Diving Armor](/Feats.aspx?ID=3056)

**Access**
Tian Xia origin

</column>

---

You've rigged your armor innovation with a gizmo that can safely synthesize two sea mines a day, holding them in a protected compartment. You must be submerged in water to use this action, and the mines only detonate if they're underwater. 

You off-load a mine onto an unoccupied square within your reach. The mine is primed to explode when a Small or larger creature moves into its square, or when you Interact to Activate your gizmo's remote detonation button, which explodes a single mine up to 50 feet away. As a 1-minute activity, you can hide a mine. Creatures can detect a hidden mine as they would any trap or hazard, using your Craft DC as the Stealth DC. If you don't Conceal the mine, its position is obvious.

Any creature in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around the mine when it detonates takes 3d4 bludgeoning damage and 1d4 piercing damage, with a basic Reflex save against your class DC. The bludgeoning damage of your mines increases by 1d4 at 8th level and every 2 levels thereafter. Any mines that haven't detonated when you make your daily preparations become inert and harmless.

**Unstable Function** You push the safety limits of your gizmo, forcing it to synthesize and launch additional sea mines, exceeding the daily limit. Add the [unstable](/Traits.aspx?ID=416) trait to Xidao Sea Mine Drop. You create up to 3 additional sea mines that you can immediately deploy in different unoccupied spaces up to 30 feet away.
name: Xidao Sea Mine Drop

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Guardian Lion Roar](/Feats.aspx?ID=7078) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=697" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 111

**Prerequisites**
[armor innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=1), [construct innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=2), or [weapon innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

Your innovation is augmented with plates shaped to resemble the gaping maws of guardian lions, which you can energize to expel a stream of sonic energy reminiscent of a lion’s powerful roar. All creatures in a 20-foot line from your innovation take 2d8 sonic damage with a basic Fortitude save against your class DC. Creatures that fail this save become [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round. The damage from this effect increases by 1d8 at 10th level and every 2 levels thereafter.

**Unstable Function** You press concealed switches in the guardian lions’ plates, extending their reach and power. Add the unstable trait to Guardian Lion Roar. The range of your roar increases to a 60-foot line. Creatures that succeed (but not critically succeed) at their save also become off-guard for 1 round. On a critical failure when attempting the flat check for this unstable action, you take sonic damage equal to your level instead of fire damage.

**Special** If your innovation is a minion, it can take this action rather than you.
name: Guardian Lion Roar

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Geobukseon Retaliation](/Feats.aspx?ID=7079) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
<trait label="Modification" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=410" />
<trait label="Unstable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=416" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 111

**Prerequisites**
[armor innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=1)

**Access**
Tian Xia origin

**Trigger**
You would take damage.

</column>

---

Your armor has a retractable spike-covered shell reminiscent of Hwanggot’s heavily armored geobukseon, or “turtle ships.” The shell unfolds across your body, giving you a +4 circumstance bonus to AC and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 2 to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage until the end of your next turn. Spiked chains propelled by small bursts of black powder also erupt from the shell, dealing 3d4 piercing damage plus 2 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (basic Reflex save against your class DC) to all creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387).
name: Geobukseon Retaliation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Celestial Cacophony](/Feats.aspx?ID=7080) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=697" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 111

**Prerequisites**
[weapon innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=3)

**Access**
Tian Xia origin

</column>

---

Your weapon contains a detonation chamber that launches fireworks, sparklers, and black powder pellets that sizzle, twist, and scatter on the ground in a cacophonous manner liable to annoy the entire Celestial Court. All creatures in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) extending from your innovation take 3d4 fire damage and 3d4 [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage with a basic Fortitude save against your class DC; both the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) and sonic damage increase by 1d4 at 14th level and every 2 levels thereafter. The area affected by the cone becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) until the start of your next turn. Creatures moving through this area must succeed at a Reflex save against your class DC or take 2d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

**Unstable Function** You rattle your weapon violently to make it belch out even more pyrotechnics. Add the [unstable](/Traits.aspx?ID=416) trait to Celestial Cacophony. The area increases to a 30-foot cone, and all damage dice increase from d4s to d8s.
name: Celestial Cacophony

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Distant Waterbird's Poise](/Feats.aspx?ID=7081) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 113

**Prerequisites**
[aloof firmament hybrid study](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=7); [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755)

**Trigger**
You hit a target with a Spellstrike.

</column>

---

You flow around your foe, using the magic of your attack to carry you away. You [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), and this movement doesn’t trigger reactions from the creature you hit. If you High Jump or Long Jump, you don’t have to perform the initial Stride (nor do you fall if you don’t Stride 10 feet). 

The lightness of your steps persists after the jump, granting you the ability to land on or walk on the surface of water or other liquids until the end of your next turn.
name: Distant Waterbird's Poise

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Heaven-Earth Encompassing Sleeves](/Feats.aspx?ID=7082) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=596" />
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 113

**Prerequisites**
[unfurling brocade hybrid study](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

Your magic extends to your garments. During your daily preparations, you can weave magic into a single suit of fine or high-fashioned fine clothing with long sleeves or a train, such as a robe or cloak. This clothing becomes a set of [sleeves of storage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3110), allowing you to store items in either the sleeves or the train (you choose which during your daily preparations). This treatment also makes the clothing more durable; its [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) increases by an amount equal to your level, and its Hit Points increase by an amount equal to twice your level.

These benefits end if you prepare a new set of clothing, if someone else attempts to wear the clothing, or if you lose ownership of the clothing. When it ends, all items stored within the clothes fall into the nearest available space.

At 9th level, the clothing becomes greater sleeves of storage instead.
name: Heaven-Earth Encompassing Sleeves

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vermilion Threads](/Feats.aspx?ID=7083) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 113

**Prerequisites**
[Arcane Cascade](/Actions.aspx?ID=756); [unfurling brocade hybrid study](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=8)

**Trigger**
You use Arcane Cascade.

</column>

---

Your Arcane Cascade sends threads to the four directions, creating a web of strings dyed by your skill and your enemies’ blood. The web fills a 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) centered on you and is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). You can ignore the difficult terrain, and the strings can support your weight, allowing you to walk on them as though you were benefiting from [fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312). 

The web lasts for 1 minute but ends early if your Arcane Cascade ends or you leave the web.

If you [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) from a spell slot while in the web, the strings glow red until the beginning of your next turn, dealing damage equal to the spell’s rank to any creature other than you that moves through the web. The damage occurs at the end of the creature’s movement, with a basic Reflex save against your spell DC. A creature can take this damage only once each round.
name: Vermilion Threads

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unsheathing the Sword-Light](/Feats.aspx?ID=7084) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 113

**Prerequisites**
[aloof firmament hybrid study](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=7); [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755)

**Requirements**
Your Spellstrike is charged, and you're wielding a one-handed weapon in the sword group.

</column>

---

Honed through 10,000 battles, your sword’s mere light can shatter ambitions and break armies. Make a melee [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755) with a sword, with a spell that isn’t a [cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=550) or [focus](/Traits.aspx?ID=511) spell. Countless copies of your sword, made of light, fall around you, dealing damage equal to double the spell’s rank to each creature within a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of the target, excluding you and the target of the Spellstrike. The damage is of the same type dealt by your sword Strike and is treated as [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) and [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920).
name: Unsheathing the Sword-Light
requirement_markdown: Your Spellstrike is charged, and you're wielding a one-handed weapon in the sword group.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cultivator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7085) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 114

**Archetype** [Cultivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=278)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)

</column>

---

Through an esoteric, often exacting, regimen of meditation, diet, and exercise, you’ve learned to transform your body’s inner workings into a crucible of planar and spiritual energies. These austerities allow you to refine your body’s vitality into qi, the foundation of all cultivation. 

You become an expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44). In addition, you gain the [_adapt self_](/Spells.aspx?ID=594) domain spell as a [focus](/Traits.aspx?ID=511) spell. It costs 1 [Focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) Point to cast a focus spell, and you start with a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) activity meditating to refine essence into qi, which circulates and refills your focus pool. Your cultivator focus spells are occult spells. You’re trained in spell attack modifier and spell DC. Your key spellcasting attribute for these spells is Wisdom.

Cultivator focus spells are treated as qi spells for prerequisites, counting the number of qi spells you possess and their effects, such as a [jiang-shi’s](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=286) Drain Qi.

<title level="2">Cultivator Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cultivator's Keen Eye](/Feats.aspx?ID=7090), [Ghost-path Epiphany](/Feats.aspx?ID=7091), [Immortal Lightness](/Feats.aspx?ID=7086), [Three Clear Breaths](/Feats.aspx?ID=7088), [Three Pecks of Dew](/Feats.aspx?ID=7092), [Wisteria-and-Peony Reunion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7089)
name: Cultivator Dedication

<summary>
Through an esoteric, often exacting, regimen of meditation, diet, and exercise, you’ve learned to transform your body’s inner workings into a crucible of planar and spiritual energies.
</summary>

You gain the [Breath Control](/Feats.aspx?ID=5129), [Diehard](/Feats.aspx?ID=5140), and [Fast Recovery](/Feats.aspx?ID=5148) feats. You must meet the prerequisites for these feats as normal. For each of these feats you already have, you can instead gain a different feat from the following list: [Canny Acumen](/Feats.aspx?ID=5130), [Fleet](/Feats.aspx?ID=5150), and [Toughness](/Feats.aspx?ID=5227).

Attuned to all arrangements of qi, you gain [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) as an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) sense with a range of 30 feet. You can also sense the presence of precious materials in the same range, which cultivators refer to as “cultivation materials.” 

When you participate in rituals, you can substitute all or part of the ritual’s cost with an equivalent value of precious materials. This applies only to costs in valuable substances like diamonds, not to rituals that require specific items to function; the GM makes the call if it’s unclear.
name: Cultivator's Keen Eye

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghost-path Epiphany](/Feats.aspx?ID=7091) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 114

**Archetype** [Cultivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=278)

**Prerequisites**
[Cultivator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7085); you aren't [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517)

</column>

---

Deviating from the orthodox path, you tread an inauspicious descent littered with flowers of death; this heretical choice will, in time, earn you the enmity and fear of more traditional cultivators. You become [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519) with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait. You also learn the [_call spirit_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=112) and [_commune_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=114) rituals, which can be used to contact only [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) or entities from the Void or the [Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36).

When you cast a ritual, you can reduce the number of secondary casters by 1 as you direct decaying fragments of your own soul, felled by your proximity to death, to assist with the ritual. When you do, you must fulfill any requirements for the secondary caster, and you attempt the secondary check normally performed by that secondary caster. You can’t replace a secondary caster who’s the target of the spell (as in the [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=108) ritual).

<title level="2">Ghost-path Epiphany leads to...</title>
[The Bitter Scholar's Promotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7093)
name: Ghost-path Epiphany

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Three Pecks of Dew](/Feats.aspx?ID=7092) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 115

**Archetype** [Cultivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=278)

**Prerequisites**
[Cultivator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7085); you aren't [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)

</column>

---

At this stage of cultivation, your body is as much spirit as flesh. While still fettered to the physical realm, your form flourishes without needing conventional food or drink, and your natural healing now rivals that brought by scalpel and suture.

You become [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519) with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait. When you perform the [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) downtime activity, you can use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) for the skill check (instead of the skills normally associated with your environment). If you do so, this activity gains the [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) trait as you subsist on ambient qi within your environment’s dew-laden air and create shelter from solidified emanations of qi.

Additionally, when you [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621), you can also [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) at the same time. If you do so, you can use Occultism for checks to Treat Wounds and disregard the need for a [healer’s kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727); this activity gains the vitality trait as you circulate healing qi through your own body or transfuse your ally with healing qi.

<title level="2">Three Pecks of Dew leads to...</title>
[The Immortal Attains the Summit](/Feats.aspx?ID=7094)
name: Three Pecks of Dew

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[The Bitter Scholar's Promotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7093) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 115

**Archetype** [Cultivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=278)

**Prerequisites**
[Ghost-path Epiphany](/Feats.aspx?ID=7091)

</column>

---

Tian Xia’s shining cities belie its empires’ long shadow of death, darkened by thousands of years of suffering and injustice. You pursue immortality not through tuft-hunting with Heaven’s dragons or their imperial brats; yours is the power to crack its corrupt wheel, for you can now release the ghost gates to expedite the dead’s revenge at being cheated of their fates. 

You cease aging and, regardless of your actual existential state, now register as an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) to effects that can detect undead (such as [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) or spiritsense). In addition, you learn [_create undead_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=117) rituals for [gashadokuro](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1860), [jiangshi](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=286), and [shui gui](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2784). On a success with one of these rituals, you gain the effects of a critical success instead.
name: The Bitter Scholar's Promotion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[The Immortal Attains the Summit](/Feats.aspx?ID=7094) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 115

**Archetype** [Cultivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=278)

**Prerequisites**
[Three Pecks of Dew](/Feats.aspx?ID=7092)

</column>

---

Your cultivation bearing glorious fruit, you behold Heaven’s will. Returning from your vision of the Holy Mountain, you become an immortal with mystic potency over the terrestrial realms of wind and water.

You cease aging and, regardless of your actual existential state, now register as a living being to effects that can detect living creatures (such as [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) or spiritsense). 

In addition, you learn the [_control weather_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=116) and [_plant growth_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=121) rituals. You can use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) instead of [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) for these rituals. On a success with one of these rituals, you gain the effects of a critical success instead.
name: The Immortal Attains the Summit

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Familiar Sage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=279)

**Prerequisites**
You have a [familiar](/Rules.aspx?ID=2121), and you're able to [cast spells](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734).

</column>

---

You’ve forged an empowered mystical bond with your familiar. You gain the [Enhanced Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=4717) feat, increasing your familiar’s number of abilities.

**Special** You can take Familiar Sage Dedication even if you haven’t yet gained three feats from the [familiar master](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=250) archetype, and you can take Familiar Master Dedication even if you haven’t yet gained three feats from the familiar sage archetype. You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained two other feats from the familiar master or familiar sage archetypes.

<title level="2">Familiar Sage Dedication leads to...</title>
[Familiar Ritualist](/Feats.aspx?ID=7099), [Familiar's Resolve](/Feats.aspx?ID=7096), [Fulu Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=7097), [Golden Dragon's Bounty](/Feats.aspx?ID=7100), [Lightning Rings' Intervention](/Feats.aspx?ID=7101), [Lightning Rings' Overcharge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7103), [Phoenix's Flight](/Feats.aspx?ID=7105), [Seal of the Golden Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=7102), [Tempest Cloud's Speed](/Feats.aspx?ID=7098), [Vexing Tempest](/Feats.aspx?ID=7104)
name: Familiar Sage Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: You have a [familiar](/Rules.aspx?ID=2121), and you're able to [cast spells](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734).

At 14th level, you or an ally gain a +3 status bonus to saving throws against these effects instead.

<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />

---

You and your familiar coordinate to channel elemental air. Your familiar gains the following ability. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Path of the Tempest;  **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) Your familiar summons winds that speed up and protect you. You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed until the end of your turn. When you [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) this turn, you don’t trigger reactions. In addition, you don’t need to Stride before a [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) this turn.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Familiar Ritualist](/Feats.aspx?ID=7099) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Familiar Sage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=279)

**Prerequisites**
[Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095); ability to cast a ritual

</column>

---

Your familiar’s magical presence can serve as an aspect of a ritual. When you cast a ritual, your familiar can serve as a secondary caster, fulfilling any requirements for the secondary caster as well as a secondary check. You can’t replace a secondary caster who’s the target of the spell.
name: Familiar Ritualist
prerequisite_markdown: [Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095); ability to cast a ritual

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Golden Dragon's Bounty](/Feats.aspx?ID=7100) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Spellshape" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=513" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Familiar Sage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=279)

**Prerequisites**
[Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095)

</column>

---

Your familiar alters the material of a spell into that of a precious metal. If the next action you use is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that deals bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, that damage is your choice of [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916), [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920), or steel for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, vulnerabilities, and the like. At 14th level, [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) is added to the list of metal choices. The spell gains the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait.

<title level="2">Golden Dragon's Bounty leads to...</title>
[Seal of the Golden Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=7102)
name: Golden Dragon's Bounty

    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lightning Rings' Intervention](/Feats.aspx?ID=7101) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=586" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Familiar Sage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=279)

**Prerequisites**
[Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095)

</column>

---

Your familiar learns to empower spells with an electrical discharge. Your familiar gains the following ability. 

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Lightning Needles;    **Requirements** You Cast a Spell that had the air or electricity traits this turn; **Effect** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) Your familiar absorbs excess electrical charge and unleashes a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of static electricity that flows into creatures’ meridians. Creatures in the emanation that fail a Fortitude save against your [spell DC](/Rules.aspx?ID=2293) become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the end of their next turn. If a creature is soaked or standing over or in water inside the emanation, it also takes 1d4 [persistent electricity damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) on a failed save.

<title level="2">Lightning Rings' Intervention leads to...</title>
[Lightning Rings' Overcharge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7103)
name: Lightning Rings' Intervention

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Seal of the Golden Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=7102) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 117

**Archetype** [Familiar Sage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=279)

**Prerequisites**
[Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095); [Golden Dragon's Bounty](/Feats.aspx?ID=7100)

**Trigger**
You would take [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage.

**Requirements**
Your familiar has the resistance ability and is adjacent to you.

</column>

---

Your familiar coils around you, protecting you with its magical resistance. You gain the benefits of your familiar’s [resistance](/Familiars.aspx?ID=122) ability until the start of your next turn. If your familiar has any upgrades to this ability, such as [major resistance](/Familiars.aspx?ID=117), you gain them as well. Once per day, if your familiar’s resistance is a different damage type, your familiar’s resistance ability changes to the appropriate type. If you already have resistance of the same type, choose the higher of the two.
name: Seal of the Golden Dragon
requirement_markdown: Your familiar has the resistance ability and is adjacent to you.

    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lightning Rings' Overcharge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7103) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=586" />

[Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095); [Lightning Rings' Intervention](/Feats.aspx?ID=7101)

</column>

---

Your familiar can adopt an electrical form to empower weapon strikes. Your familiar gains the following ability. 

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Lightning Armillary;     **Effect** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) Your familiar flies around and around an adjacent ally’s weapon or hand, dissolving into multiple rings of lightning. Until the beginning of your next turn, the affected weapon or unarmed attack deals an additional 1d6 electricity damage, or 1d8 electricity damage if you [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) with the air or electricity trait this turn. Your familiar remains in lightning ring form, preventing it from being targeted or taking any actions.
name: Lightning Rings' Overcharge

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=527" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />

[Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095); [Tempest Cloud's Speed](/Feats.aspx?ID=7098)

</column>

---

Your familiar channels elemental air around it to unleash a disruptive gust that sends creatures flying. If the next action you use is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that has the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) trait, all creatures within a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of your familiar must attempt a Reflex save against your spell DC or be pushed 10 feet away. On a critical failure, they’re also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Phoenix's Flight](/Feats.aspx?ID=7105) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 117

**Archetype** [Familiar Sage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=279)

**Prerequisites**
[Familiar Sage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7095)

**Requirements**
You're adjacent to or sharing the same space as your familiar.

</column>

---

You and your familiar learn the secrets of elemental fire, allowing you to merge together to become a legendary creature—a [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137). You can cast monstrosity form as an innate [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) spell once per day, except you can transform only into a phoenix, and your familiar must be adjacent to you before you [Cast the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734). When you Cast the Spell, your familiar merges into your form. While transformed, you gain the Blazing Conflagration action.  

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Blazing Conflagration;    **Requirements** You’re transformed into phoenix form by the monstrosity form spell granted by this feat; **Effect** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) You dismiss phoenix form while a fiery corona erupts from you as searing light. Each creature in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) takes 16d6 fire damage with a basic Fortitude save against your [spell DC](/Rules.aspx?ID=2293); creatures that critically fail are [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round. You gain 8d6 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321). At 18th level and again at 20th level, the burst deals an additional 2d6 fire damage, and you gain an additional 1d6 temporary Hit Points.
name: Phoenix's Flight
requirement_markdown: You're adjacent to or sharing the same space as your familiar.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rushing Goat Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7106) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 119

**Archetype** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=255) (Level 4)

**Requirements**
You're [unarmored](/Armor.aspx?ID=38).

</column>

---

You enter the stance of an unruly goat, lowering your body and preparing to butt heads with your foes. The only [Strikes](/Rules.aspx?ID=2895) you can make are ramming horn attacks. These strikes deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage, are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and have the [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits. When you make a ramming horn attack and your previous action was to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), Stride, or [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), you gain a circumstance bonus to damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice. You can make ramming horn Strikes while Climbing even if you don’t have a free hand.

<title level="2">Rushing Goat Stance leads to...</title>
[Five-gods Ram](/Feats.aspx?ID=7112), [Momentous Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7109)
name: Rushing Goat Stance
requirement_markdown: You're [unarmored](/Armor.aspx?ID=38).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Twisting Petal Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7107) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 119

**Archetype** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=255) (Level 4)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38)

**Requirements**
You're [unarmored](/Armor.aspx?ID=38).

</column>

---

Your body moves like a petal twisting on the wind, confounding your opponents while using their energy against them. You can make gale blossom strikes that deal 1d6 slashing damage. These strikes are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits. While in Twisting Petal Stance, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) and a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC to avoid being Shoved. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390) and a +2 circumstance bonus to your Perception DC to resist an opponent’s Feint attempt.

<title level="2">Twisting Petal Stance leads to...</title>
[Scattering in Spring](/Feats.aspx?ID=7111), [Whirling in the Summer Storm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7113)
name: Twisting Petal Stance
requirement_markdown: You're [unarmored](/Armor.aspx?ID=38).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kaiju Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7110) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 120

**Access**
You've encountered a  kaiju  and survived.

**Requirements**
You're [unarmored](/Armor.aspx?ID=38).

</column>

---

Your encounter with one of the rare forces of nature known as [kaiju](/Traits.aspx?ID=383) has imbued you with knowledge of a martial art that allows you to emulate a fraction of their power. When you enter Kaiju Stance, you become Large and are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1. The only Strikes you can make are shattering earth attacks. These strikes deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage, are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and have the [backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=545), [fatal](/Traits.aspx?ID=597) d12, [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits. On a critical success with a shattering earth attack, all creatures other than you that are within 10 feet of the target, including the target itself, take 1 point of bludgeoning [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage per weapon damage die.

While in Kaiju Stance, you ignore [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).

<title level="2">Kaiju Stance leads to...</title>
[Wake of Devastation](/Feats.aspx?ID=7115), [World-breaking Footfall](/Feats.aspx?ID=7114)
name: Kaiju Stance
requirement_markdown: You're [unarmored](/Armor.aspx?ID=38).

If the second Strike is successful, you immediately knock the target [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), but if the second Strike is a failure or if you’re unable to complete all the required actions, you become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.     level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Apparition Sense](/Feats.aspx?ID=7120) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=836" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 22

</column>

---

You can see and interact with things others can’t. You have apparition sight, an imprecise sense that allows you to detect the presence of [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) [spirits](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=622), and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) within 30 feet of you.

You can allow a spirit or undead otherwise incapable of speech to speak through you as long as you are in direct contact with it. As an activity that takes 10 minutes, you can act as a link between disembodied souls and their mortal bodies. As long as you are in contact with both a spirit and a living body that belonged to it in life during that entire time, the spirit can use you to return to that body; this does not allow you to bring the dead back to life, but can assist in restoring a disembodied soul to a still-living body. If the body is occupied by another spirit or soul, that entity must succeed at a Will save against your spell DC or be cast from the body when its original owner is returned.

<title level="2">Apparition Sense leads to...</title>
[Spirit Walk](/Feats.aspx?ID=7137)

    pfs="Standard"

[Relinquish Control](/Feats.aspx?ID=7122) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Apparition" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=837" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

Your turn begins.

</column>

---

Your apparition takes over and shields you from outside influence. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a +4 status bonus on saves against spells and effects that give you the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) condition or attempt to influence your actions (such as [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), or a [nosoi’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3147) haunting melody). However, the only actions you can take are to [Recall Knowledge](/Rules.aspx?ID=2638), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), [Cast an apparition Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), Cast a vessel Spell, [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) a vessel spell, or use an action that has the [apparition](/Traits.aspx?ID=837) trait. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus on all Recall Knowledge checks made using Lore skills granted by your attuned apparitions.

**Special** This feat requires a particularly strong bond with a specific apparition to learn. Choose one apparition you have access to; once you learn this feat, you must always choose that apparition as one of the apparitions you attune to each day.

[Instinctive Maneuvers](/Feats.aspx?ID=7136)

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grasping Spirits Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7125) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=836" />
<trait label="Apparition" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=837" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Spellshape" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=513" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 23

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

</column>

---

Gaining substance from your magic, your apparitions increase the range of your spells, which then pull your enemy closer. If the next action you use is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that has a range and targets one creature, increase that spell’s range by 30 feet. As is standard for increasing spell ranges, if the spell normally has a range of touch, you extend its range to 30 feet. In addition to the normal effects of the spell, your apparitions briefly take on semi-physical forms and attempt to drag the target toward you. The target must attempt a Fortitude saving throw against your spell DC; on a failure, it is pulled up to 30 feet directly toward you.
name: Grasping Spirits Spell

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiritual Expansion Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7126) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=836" />
<trait label="Apparition" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=837" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Spellshape" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=513" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 24

</column>

---

Your apparitions manifest to scatter the energy of your spell. If the next action you use is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that has an area of a [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), or [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) and does not have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line. 

You can also use this feat to increase the radius of an [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) spell with a duration by 5 feet by dedicating your primary apparition to maintaining the spellshape; dedicating the apparition to the spell prevents you from using the apparition’s vessel spell, apparition skills, or avatar form for the duration of the modified spell.
name: Spiritual Expansion Spell

When you gain the third apparition class feature, this DC is reduced to 13, and when you gain the fourth apparition class feature, this DC is reduced to 10.

When you end the movement, the turbulent spirits you’re attuned to reward you for acting in an appropriately fierce manner: you and each ally in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain temporary Hit Points equal to half your level if you successfully Shoved at least one enemy, or equal to your level if you succeeded at Shoving both. These temporary Hit Points last until the beginning of your next turn.

You and allies in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain a +2 status bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks about [spirits](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=622), and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). While you’re [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630) or [Detecting Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2624) in exploration mode, this bonus also applies to AC and saves against reactions any of you trigger from haunts and spirits. During your first turn in an encounter, you and allies in the aura have resistance equal to half your level against damage dealt by haunts or spirits.

---

If your next action is to [cast a cantrip or a spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that is at least 2 ranks lower than the highest-rank spell slot you have, you can draw power from one of your attuned apparitions to reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action). The chosen apparition is dispersed until you can re-attune to it at your next daily preparations, but you can’t disperse an apparition in this way if you would no longer be attuned to any apparitions afterward.

**Special** This can only be used on a cantrip or spell from the class matching the one you gained this feat from.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Apparition Cloud](/Feats.aspx?ID=7141) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=836" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=654" />
<trait label="Wandering" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=843" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 26

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) After using Apparition Cloud, your familiar reincorporates at the start of your next turn and all effects of the feat end._

**Prerequisites**
[Spirit Familiar (Animist)](/Feats.aspx?ID=7123)

**Trigger**
You are targeted by an attack.

**Requirements**
Your familiar is adjacent to you or in your space.

</column>

---

With a mere thought, you discorporate your familiar into a thousand shards of spiritual magic, protecting both it and yourself from physical harm while making it difficult for enemies to move near you. You are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from the triggering attack. Until the start of your next turn, your familiar can’t be targeted and your enemies treat all spaces adjacent to you as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).
name: Apparition Cloud
requirement_markdown: Your familiar is adjacent to you or in your space.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=836" />
<trait label="Apparition" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=837" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=654" />
<trait label="Wandering" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=843" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 26

**Trigger**
You would be detected by an enemy [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) you, or an enemy would succeed at a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check against a spell making you [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96).

**Requirements**
Your attuned apparition grants Hunting [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Underworld Lore as one of its apparition skills.

</column>

---

Your apparition possesses a furtive and elusive nature that can influence and blend with your spiritual energy to form a protective and nearly impenetrable shroud of nondetection around you. The enemy must reroll the triggering check and take the lower result.
name: Shadows Within Shadows
requirement_markdown: Your attuned apparition grants Hunting [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Underworld Lore as one of its apparition skills.

You focus your [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) magic to allow your apparitions to work in concert using the magic from one to weaken an enemy against the power of the next. Whenever you succeed at a spell attack with an apparition spell or vessel spell, or when a creature fails its save against such a spell, you gain a +2 status bonus to your spell attack modifier against that creature with spells granted by any of your other apparitions, and it takes a –2 status penalty to its saves against such spells. These benefits last until the end of your next turn.
name: Cardinal Guardians

    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Forest's Heart](/Feats.aspx?ID=7146) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=836" />
<trait label="Apparition" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=837" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
<trait label="Wandering" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=843" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 26

**Requirements**
Your attuned apparition grants Forest [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Herbalism Lore as one of its apparition skills.

</column>

---

You adopt a stance that allows your apparitions to channel spiritual energy down through you to inhabit roots, control vines, and command plant-life from the ground you stand on to fight on your behalf. You call forth roots, vines, or other plant growth native to the region and can use them to make unarmed attacks. These deal 4d8 bludgeoning damage, are in the [brawling group](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4), and have the [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619), and [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) 30 feet traits. Attacks made with these unarmed attacks don’t gain additional damage from [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) runes, but can benefit from the item bonus to attack rolls and [property runes](/Rules.aspx?ID=3168) of [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086) you wear.
name: Forest's Heart
requirement_markdown: Your attuned apparition grants Forest [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Herbalism Lore as one of its apparition skills.

Your apparitions share with you the monstrous nature of those creatures who dwell in deep seas or ancient woods. You add [_monstrosity form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1608) to your apparition spell repertoire, allowing you to cast it with your apparition spellcasting.

**Special** If you are attuned to a stalker in darkened boughs, add the phoenix form from [_monstrosity form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1608) to your available [_darkened forest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2143)  options when you cast the spell heightened to 8th rank.

If you are attuned to a lurker in devouring dark, add the cave worm and sea serpent forms to your [_devouring dark form _](/Spells.aspx?ID=2140) options as a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect when you cast the spell heightened to 8th rank.

---

Your attuned apparition gathers all of the energy it can through your shared bond and uses that power to disperse itself while channeling the energy back into you, saving your life. You can use Spirit’s Sacrifice even while [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) or otherwise unable to act. Choose one apparition you have attuned; you do not die, your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition is reduced to 1 if it would be higher, and you regain a number of Hit Points equal to twice your level. The chosen apparition is dispersed until you can re-attune to it at your next daily preparations.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eternal Guide](/Feats.aspx?ID=7152) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=836" />
<trait label="Apparition" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=837" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 26

**Frequency**
once per week

</column>

---

Your attuned apparitions recognize that you still have essential work to do and refuse to allow you to pass on, gathering up your essence to carry your soul back to the community you call home. Choose a single settlement with a population of at least 100 people. Whenever you die and are not returned to life within 24 hours by other means, as long as the settlement still exists and there are no other claims on your soul (such as a contract with a [devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=575)) and nothing trapping your soul (such as being slain by a critical hit from a [vilderavn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3229)), your attuned apparition magically transports your soul, remains, and all of the items you had worn or carried at the time of your death (so long as they have not been otherwise recovered) back to the chosen settlement, where you are reborn. The nature of this rebirth varies between animists; some magically reconstitute and reinvigorate their physical forms to return to life in their original bodies, while others pass their knowledge and power on to a new inheritor who effectively takes their place in the grand story of existence. When returned to your original physical form in this manner, you are brought back in your physical prime, restored to youthful health and vigor. This effectively makes you immune to dying of old age so long as you are not killed by violence less than a week before you would have died of old age.

Regardless of the exact nature of your rebirth, you or your inheritor are fully restored with your level, experience, and equipment intact one week after your death. If you choose an inheritor and their ancestry is different than yours, your attribute scores do not change but you can freely retrain your ancestry feats and any other feats that relied on them as prerequisites as part of your rebirth.

There is no limit to the number of times you can be reborn, but if you die before a week has passed since your last rebirth, neither you nor your attuned apparitions have recovered enough energy to complete the process again, and your soul passes to whatever final reward it has earned.
name: Eternal Guide

<title

    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[True Channel Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7153) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=836" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 26

</column>

---

You are a true conduit for spiritual power, able to unleash the greatest spells. You gain a single 10th-rank spell slot and can prepare a spell in that slot using animist spellcasting. You can’t use 10th-rank slots with abilities that give you more spell slots or that let you cast spells without expending spell slots.
name: True Channel Spell

    pfs="Standard"

[Energized Spark](/Feats.aspx?ID=7154) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 35

</column>

---

The energy of your spirit manifests as crackling lightning, the chill of winter, or the power of an element. Choose one of the following traits: [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) (slashing), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) (bludgeoning), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) (slashing), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) (bludgeoning), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) (piercing). You can choose for any [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage dealt by your exemplar abilities to instead gain the trait and deal the corresponding damage type.

**Special** You can select this feat multiple times, choosing a different damage type each time.
name: Energized Spark

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Twin Stars](/Feats.aspx?ID=7156) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Ikon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=840" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 35

</column>

---

Your divine spark embodies a primordial duality, and your ikon splits itself accordingly into two corresponding halves. You can use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to split the imbued ikon into two copies of itself, which both gain the [twin](/Traits.aspx?ID=717) trait; these copies are identical except for one mirrored feature, such as a sun motif on one and a moon motif on another. You can use this ability again to fuse the two halves back into their original form. As these are both manifestations of the same object, your divine spark empowers the two halves as if they were a single ikon.
name: Twin Stars

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vow of Mortal Defiance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7157) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=642" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 35

**Prerequisites**
You are not [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519) with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait.

</column>

---

Having seen the wreckage left by the gods and their servitors as they play in their great war of good and evil, you’ve come to the only reasonable conclusion: they all must be cut from their silken thrones. You swear a vow to defeat one creature within 60 feet that has the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait. The first time each round that you deal damage to that creature, you deal an additional 1d6 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage. You can’t use Vow of Mortal Defiance again until you or the target is defeated, flees, or the encounter ends.

**Special** If you take this feat, you can’t become sanctified with the holy or unholy trait. Retraining out of this feat typically requires a major change of philosophy.
name: Vow of Mortal Defiance
prerequisite_markdown: You are not [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519) with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait.

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 35 --- By channeling your divinity into a creature along with a strike, you can disrupt their ability to recover. The imbued ikon gains the following ability. **Immanence** When you successfully damage an enemy with the ikon, a marking appears around the wound, painted in the red of mortal blood and the gold of divine ichor. When the target would regain Hit Points, such as from a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) effect or an ability like [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) or [regeneration](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72), it must attempt a Will save against your class DC to determine the effects, and then the marking fades. The marking otherwise fades after 1 minute. **Success** The creature regains the full number of Hit Points that would be healed.
**Failure** The creature regains only half the number of Hit Points as the contradictory energies swirl within it.
**Critical Failure** The creature doesn’t regain any Hit Points.
name: Red-Gold Mortality

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Only the Worthy](/Feats.aspx?ID=7161) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Ikon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=840" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 36

</column>

---

You can designate your ikons as movable by only your own hand, leaving them fixed in place as surely as if they were lodged in stone. Whenever you Release an ikon, you can spend an action to command it to remain motionless. While motionless, the ikon can be moved only if 8,000 pounds of pressure are applied to it or if a creature uses [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) to Force Open the ikon with a DC equal to your class DC. You can Release your ikon over an adjacent prone enemy to hold them down with the ikon’s motionlessness—while so Released, you can’t use the ikon, but the enemy must succeed at the Athletics check to Stand or to move. The ikon automatically flies back to your hand when the effect is broken or if you spend an Interact action to hold out a hand and draw it back.
name: Only the Worthy

A creature that critically fails its saving throw is deafened for 1 minute. At 6th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d4 spirit damage and 1d4 sonic damage.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Battle Hymn to the Lost](/Feats.aspx?ID=7168) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 37

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[of verse unbroken](/Epithets.aspx?ID=9) or [peerless under heaven](/Epithets.aspx?ID=10)

</column>

---

Your movements in combat are an artistic dirge to those spirits who could not fight to see another day. You make a Strike. Regardless of whether the Strike succeeds, spirits of warriors who died gloriously in battle surge out to attack with you. They appear in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) if you make a melee Strike or in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around your target if you made a ranged Strike. Each enemy in the area takes 2d10 damage with a basic Reflex save against your class DC; you choose whether the damage is bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing. At 12th level and every 4 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d10. 

As they join the battle, the spirits call your fallen allies back to the fight. Any of your allies in the area who are dying regain Hit Points equal to the damage you rolled. This is a divine, healing, vitality effect.
name: Battle Hymn to the Lost

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Raise Island](/Feats.aspx?ID=7169) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 37

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[born of the bones of the earth](/Epithets.aspx?ID=7) or [restless as the tides](/Epithets.aspx?ID=11)

</column>

---

You churn the sea and fish up stone so your enemies together can receive their punishment. Each enemy in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or be swept up to 15 feet to another location of your choice within the affected area and become [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn. Then, if you’re on a surface, a pillar of earth rises underneath you and lasts for 1 minute. The pillar is 10 feet tall, and its surface includes your space and a 5-foot emanation around you. After the pillar rises, you can attempt a melee Strike against one enemy in reach.
name: Raise Island
prerequisite_markdown: [born of the bones of the earth](/Epithets.aspx?ID=7) or [restless as the tides](/Epithets.aspx?ID=11)

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 37 **Frequency** once per hour **Prerequisites** [whose cry is thunder](/Epithets.aspx?ID=12) or [dancer in the seasons](/Epithets.aspx?ID=8) --- You hold your arms out, and the fury of the seasons comes to your jubilant embrace. A storm spirals out from you, dealing 5d8 damage (see below) to each creature in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a basic Reflex save against your class DC. At 10th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d8. The emanation is quartered into four non-overlapping cones, each of a different season, which must be arranged clockwise from spring, to summer, to fall, to winter. Each cone has different traits, damage type, and a different effect to a creature that critically fails its saving throw; a creature large enough to be in multiple seasons can choose which it is affected by.
• **Spring** (electricity) Spring lightning deals electricity damage. Creatures who critically fail are left numb, becoming clumsy 2 until the end of their next turn.
• **Summer** (water) A summer monsoon deals bludgeoning damage. Creatures who critically fail are knocked prone by hurricane winds.
• **Fall** (emotion, mental, wood) Falling leaves deal slashing damage. Creatures who critically fail are gripped by melancholy, becoming off-guard until the end of their next turn.
• **Winter** (cold) A blizzard deals cold damage. Creatures who critically fail are stupefied 2 until the end of their next turn as the cold numbs their senses.
name: Rejoice in Solstice Storm
prerequisite_markdown: [whose cry is thunder](/Epithets.aspx?ID=12) or [dancer in the seasons](/Epithets.aspx?ID=8)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Breath of Vital Ash](/Feats.aspx?ID=7171) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Ikon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=840" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 38

</column>

---

Reddish ash stains your ikon, impossible to clean off. Every time you exhale, a tinge of red and a scent of spiced smoke float upon your breath. The imbued ikon gains the following ability.

Transcendence—Mark the Center <actions string="Single Action" /> (transcendence) You hold a hand to your lips and exhale, releasing enchanted red ash in a 15-foot cone. The ash clings to the vital areas of each enemy in the area for 1 minute. When a creature marked by the ash is hit and dealt damage by a Strike, it takes an additional 4d6 precision or vitality damage, then its mark ends. The damage type is whichever is worse for the creature, as determined by the GM. At 13th level and every 3 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d6.
name: Breath of Vital Ash

    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fish from the Falls' Edge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7173) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 38

**Frequency**
once per day

**Trigger**
A living creature within 30 feet would die.

**Requirements**
Your divine spark is empowering one of your ikons.

</column>

---

Seeing your ally fall, you let out a cry, sending your divine spark to them temporarily to keep them from tumbling down the River of Souls. You prevent the triggering creature from dying and restore 5d8 Hit Points to them. The ally is invigorated by the touch of your divine power, gaining a +1 status bonus to their saving throws and AC for 1 round. Your divine spark remains with your ally, preventing you from gaining any of your ikons’ immanence effects until the start of your next turn, when it returns to an ikon of your choice.
name: Fish from the Falls' Edge
requirement_markdown: Your divine spark is empowering one of your ikons.

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 39 --- Your ikons bond like a pair of one-winged birds. This feat can be imbued in either a single ikon with multiple copies, such as one produced by the shadow sheath or with the [Twin Stars](/Feats.aspx?ID=7156) feat, or two separate ikons (bypassing the normal limit per ikon feat). **Immanence** As long as you hold one ikon, the other will return to find its partner. Both ikons gain the [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2845) rune.
**Transcendence—Rejoin in Flight** (transcendence) You throw your weapon ikons to either side of a creature, and they seek each other out, converging on the target. Make two thrown Strikes against a target within the first range increment of both your weapon ikons. This counts as two attacks toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn’t increase until after you’ve made both attacks. The target is flanked for both attacks (typically making it off guard against both attacks). If both Strikes hit, the target also takes an additional die of weapon damage. The weapons then return to your hand.
name: Mated Birds in Paired Flight

Even when your ikon is not empowered, you can shrink it to a miniature size (with negligible Bulk) and keep it tucked behind an ear, in your hair, or in a similar discreet location. You can shrink your weapon or regrow it to its normal size as part of drawing it, putting it away, swapping it, or picking it up. **Immanence** Your ikon gains the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) trait. If it already had the reach trait, it instead increases your reach by an additional 10 feet, instead of the usual additional 5 feet.
**Transcendence—Topple the Pillar of Heaven** (transcendence) Your ikon extends to an impossible length, striking all in its way. You deal 6d8 damage to all creatures in a 60-foot line, with a basic Reflex save against your class DC. The damage type matches that of your weapon. At 14th level and every two levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d8.
name: Compliant Gold

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Extract Vow of Nonviolence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7177) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 39

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) When using Exact Vow of Nonviolence on an unattended item, the DC is a standard DC for the item's level._

</column>

---

You entice or threaten a weapon, making it swear not to harm you. Attempt a Diplomacy check to Request or an Intimi[dation ](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393)check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) against one weapon within 60 feet—even though most weapons are inanimate and mindless, your words somehow still can affect it as if it were living and you shared a language. The DC for this check is the Will DC of the creature wielding the weapon or a moderate DC for the weapon’s level if it’s unattended. If the weapon is an intelligent item, use its Will DC. Regardless of the result of the save, the weapon is then temporarily immune to Extract Vow of Nonviolence for 1 day. 

**Critical Success** The object swears not to hurt you. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to all damage dealt by that specific weapon as the weapon attempts to mitigate harm to you. The resistance is equal to half your level and lasts for 10 minutes.

**Success** As critical success, but the remaining duration decreases by 1 minute each time the weapon hits you (even if it deals no damage).

**Special** When you gain this feat, choose cold iron, duskwood, dawnsilver, or silver. For some reason—perhaps because you underestimate this material, or because you think it a friend—you are forever unable to use Extract Vow of Nonviolence on objects composed of the chosen material.
name: Extract Vow of Nonviolence

>
[Warped by Rage](/Feats.aspx?ID=7179) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Ikon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=840" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=657" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 39

</column>

---

Rage courses through your body, transforming you into a beast of battle. The imbued ikon gains the following abilities. 

**Immanence** You’re affected by a 4th-rank [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514) spell. You can choose to forgo this effect when your body ikon becomes empowered

**Transcendence—Spasm of the Berserker** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842)) Your body warps beyond recognition into a powerful, one eyed form. You’re affected by a 4th-rank enlarge spell and a 6th-rank [_moon frenzy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1609) spell, which both last until the end of your next turn. You can’t attempt to end the moon frenzy early.

If this ikon becomes empowered again, you can extend Spasm of the Berserker’s duration until the end of your next turn. While you’re already affected by Spasm of the Berserker, the transcend action becomes a free action to extend its duration until the end of your next turn.
name: Warped by Rage

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 40 --- You demand a battle against your chosen enemy. The imbued ikon gains the following abilities. **Immanence** Whenever an enemy successfully Strikes you with a melee weapon, you gain a +2 status bonus to your AC against the next attack from that enemy before the start of your next turn.
**Transcendence—Only You and I** (concentrate, transcendence) You draw your foe into a fated conflict—one you know you’ll win. Choose an enemy within your reach. Until the end of your next turn, that enemy gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack rolls it makes against you, but it must attempt a Will save against your class DC whenever it tries to move away from you, Strike a target other than you, or Cast a Spell that doesn’t include you as a target. On a failure, the action or actions related to the failed attempt is disrupted. Whenever the enemy misses you with a Strike while this effect is active, you heal 1d8+5 Hit Points.
name: Destined Victory

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 41 --- Your blood turns a divine gold, shining in moments its spilled and begetting new life. The imbued ikon gains the following effects. **Immanence** If your divine spark remains in your body ikon for 10 continuous minutes, you regrow one damaged or ruined limb or organ.
**Transcendence—Shed the Mortal Skin** (healing, light, transcendence, vitality) Like the serpent, that most ancient of immortals, you shed your skin, revealing a glowing divine form. If you are currently taking persistent fire or acid damage, you immediately end the effect. Until the beginning of your next turn, you shed bright light in a 20 foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) like a torch. The next time you are dealt damage by an enemy before the beginning of your next turn, you shed glowing ichor in a square adjacent to you, and where it hits the earth, a glowing, thorned herb grows. A creature other than you can pluck and consume the herb with an Interact action to regain a number of Hit Points equal to half of the damage you were dealt by the attack that sprouted the herb.
name: Gift of the Immortal Herb

Your ikon gains a pin, stripe, or similar marking showing you as a master of your craft. The imbued ikon gains the following ability. 

**Transcendence—A Challenge for Heroes** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (concentrate, mental, spirit, transcendence) You declare an enemy within 60 feet to be a heroic test. For 1 minute, that enemy deals an additional 10 spirit damage to you with each successful Strike or spell it targets you with. Whenever that enemy deals spirit damage to you, each ally who has the enemy in melee reach can attempt a [Reactive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2256) against it, even if the ally doesn’t normally have that reaction. If an ally successfully slays the enemy with a Reactive Strike due to this effect, that ally immediately gains temporary Hit Points equal to your level, which persist for 1 minute.
name: Mark of The Sage

 **Transcendence—Skirt the Underworld** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (concentrate, illusion, incapacitation, transcendence) You skirt into the world of the dead, becoming [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) for 1 round, then Fly up to your Speed. Each enemy adjacent to you at any point during this movement must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute (or permanently on a critical failure). For each creature blinded by this effect, the duration of the granted invisibility extends by 1 round. Undead and other creatures with strong ties to the world of the dead, such as psychopomps, are immune to this effect.
name: Shroud of Ghosts
