markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Strike Rivers, Seize Winds](/Feats.aspx?ID=7187) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 41

**Frequency**
once per hour

</column>

---

Through sheer physical force, you can wrestle a river, lasso the winds, punch a hole to the molten center of the planet, or create other environmental disturbances via nothing but your own strength. You can create each of the following spell effects at the listed rank, but you can’t create effects more than once per hour and you can’t perform a given effect more than once per day: **4th** [_hydraulic torrent_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), **5th** [_control water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473), **6th** [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), **7th** [_volcanic eruption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1746), **8th** [_punishing winds_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1643).

Creating the effects requires the same type and number of actions as Casting the Spell, and they use the higher of your class DC or spell DC when appropriate. Because you are creating these effects with your godly might rather than Casting a Spell, they can’t be dispelled or counteracted.

<title level="2">Strike Rivers, Seize Winds leads to...</title>
[Remake the World](/Feats.aspx?ID=7194)
name: Strike Rivers, Seize Winds

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Branched Tree Of Pain](/Feats.aspx?ID=7188) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Ikon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=840" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 41

</column>

---

The blade of your ikon takes one or more notches, capable of dealing mortal pain to your foes.

**Immanence** When you Strike with the ikon, you critically succeed if you roll a 19 on the die as long as that result would be a success. This has no effect on a 19 if the result would be a failure.

**Transcendence**—Plant Thirty Barbs <actions string="Single Action" /> ([death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842)) You snap off a fragment of your ikon within your opponent’s body, which threatens to burst into numerous barbs. Make a Strike with your ikon. If successful, barbs fill the target, dealing two die of weapon damage the first time the creature uses a move action on each of its turns; the barbs are made out of the same material as your weapon ikon and bypass resistances and such accordingly (for instance, a cold iron spear would create cold iron barbs). The barbs last for 1d4 rounds. If the target dies while the barbs are still in their body, the barbs burst forth in a forest of thorns covering the creature’s space, which are greater difficult terrain and deal piercing damage equal to half your level for each square that is moved into.
name: Branched Tree Of Pain

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eternity-Incinerating Blaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=7189) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Ikon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=840" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 42

</column>

---

The blaze of your divine spark is hot enough that it burns in not just the present, but the past and future as well. Whenever you score a critical hit with the imbued ikon, the target becomes doomed 1 or increases its doomed condition by 1. If the target’s maximum dying value is reduced to 0 by this ability, it immediately dies in a flash of spiritual fire that reduces its body to ash. This effect applies even when the ikon is not empowered. The ikon also gains the following transcendence ability.

**Transcendence—Burn out of Time** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (spirit, transcendence, void) An impossible amount of energy blazes in a sphere above you before compressing itself into your weapon. Strike one creature. The Strike deals an additional 3d8 spirit damage and 3d8 void damage. If the creature is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it’s immediately killed and reduced to ash, not in the present, but in the recent past, erasing the consequences of their recent actions. If a creature died within the last round as a result of the incinerated target’s actions, that creature is returned to life with 5d8 Hit Points at the location that it died as its death is retroactively undone.
name: Eternity-Incinerating Blaze

The imbued ikon gains the following abilities. **Immanence** (light, teleportation) Your weapon scatters rainbow-colored light across nearby surroundings, each a possible destination. When you successfully Strike an enemy, you can choose to teleport to an unoccupied location within 10 feet as a free action.
**Transcendence—Fleeting Arc through Heaven and Earth** (light, spirit, teleportation, transcendence) You swing your weapon in a wide arc, releasing a blazing rainbow that deals 9d6 spirit damage and 9d6 damage of the same type as your weapon to all enemies in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), with a basic Fortitude save against your class DC. As the rainbow ripples out, you use it as a bridge to teleport to any location within the effect’s area.
name: Seven-Colored Cosmic Bridge

The imbued ikon gains the following abilities. **Immanence** (darkness) On a successful critical hit with the weapon, the weapon casts 2nd-rank darkness centered on the target of the attack.
**Transcendence—Break the Sun’s Legs** (cold, concentrate, darkness, transcendence) You make a slashing motion over the brightest source of light, whether it’s a torch or the sun in the sky. A gash appears over the light source, visible to all, and devours the light. The entire area within a 120 foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) around you is affected by a 9th-rank darkness spell for 1 minute. The stolen light is channeled to your eyes, granting you greater [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) for the duration of the effect.
name: Sunwrecker

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cutting Without Blade](/Feats.aspx?ID=7192) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 43

</column>

---

While tales of your divine ikons have spread far and wide, you’ve realized that, as they are all manifestations of your soul, the object itself is unnecessary. Your ikons disintegrate into golden light. Any ikon feats you’ve taken now apply to any applicable ikon you have, not just one, and you can immediately retrain any ikon feats you selected more than once.

Each day during your daily preparations, you can select one ikon feat of 16th level or lower and gain it temporarily for that day. You can place your divine spark into any object in your possession, even a nonthreatening object like a single strand of grass, to transform it into a fully functional copy of your ikon made out of pure divine radiance. You can do this as a free action immediately before or after Striking with or otherwise using the ikon.
name: Cutting Without Blade

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

once per day

</column>

---

Your divine spark is now a bonfire in your soul, bringing you to the very cusp of undying immortality. You cease aging. When you would die for any reason, you can Spark Transcendence as a free action that can be taken at any time, regardless of your current condition. You reach for your divine spark and seize it in your hand to survive at 0 Hit Points, purging yourself of any negative conditions. Invigorated by this return to life, you then heal yourself for half of your total Hit Points, Stand, instantly summon any held ikons to your hand, and Shift your Immanence to any of your ikons.
name: Reach for Immortality

</title>

<traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Strike Rivers, Seize Winds](/Feats.aspx?ID=7187)

</column>

---

For the briefest of moments, you can reach into the realm of true gods, gaining the power to shape reality to your will. Once per day, you can create the effects of a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell of 8th level or lower. The spell must be common or one to which you otherwise have access, and it costs as many actions as it would typically take to cast. Use your class DC in place of any necessary spell DC and your class DC –10 in place of any necessary counteract modifier or spell attack modifier. Because you are creating these effects with your godly might rather than Casting a Spell, they can’t be dispelled or counteracted, like Strike Rivers, Seize Winds.
name: Remake the World

    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aeonbound](/Feats.aspx?ID=7195) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Lineage" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=641" />
<trait label="Nephilim" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=518" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 52

</column>

---

Your lineage is influenced or directly tied to the supernatural inhabitants of the Perfect City of Axis, a realm of pure and absolute order. This energy suffuses your body and helps it maintain coherency, resisting effects that would undermine your function or mar your otherwise orderly form. You and your allies can [Treat your Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) without a [healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727). Once per day, when someone rolls a failure or a critical failure on a check to Treat your Wounds, you can focus on your internal cohesion to increase the degree of success by one step.

<title level="2">Aeonbound leads to...</title>
[Channel the Godmind](/Feats.aspx?ID=7217), [Impose Order](/Feats.aspx?ID=7216), [Intuitive Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=7200), [Methodical Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7213), [Resilient Physiology](/Feats.aspx?ID=7207)
name: Aeonbound

[Battleblooded](/Feats.aspx?ID=7196) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

---

You descend from those whose lives were touched by legendary einherjars, who were crafted from the souls of mighty warriors slain in battle and chosen by valkyries to serve as foot soldiers to many gods. You may even be the distant mortal descendant of an actual valkyrie. You are trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40). If you were already trained in Intimidation (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also gain the [Intimidating Glare](/Feats.aspx?ID=5162) skill feat.

<title level="2">Battleblooded leads to...</title>
[Arise, ye Worthy!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7215), [Call to Battle](/Feats.aspx?ID=7205), [Glory and Valor!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7211)
name: Battleblooded

    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"

</title>

<trait label="Nephilim" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=518" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

You bear a grudge, inherited from a blasphemous asura forebear in your bloodline, against a particular deity and their works. You gain the Find Fault reaction.

**Find Fault** <actions string="Reaction" /> (concentrate) **Trigger** You attempt a saving throw against a spell or magic effect but haven’t rolled yet; Effect You find some kind of fault with the magic, using that flaw to protect yourself from the effect. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your saving throw against the triggering effect, which increases to a +2 circumstance bonus if the effect is [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) and originates from a worshipper of the deity you chose for your grudge.

<title level="2">Faultspawn leads to...</title>
[Towering Presence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7208)
name: Faultspawn

Your body emits tiny particles of extraplanar matter, such as crystalline motes or smoky vapor, that surround you in a haze. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), though you can’t use this concealment to Hide or Sneak, as normal for concealment where your position is still obvious. These particles stay within an inch of your skin, move with you, and last for a number of rounds equal to half your level.

---

Your ordered physiology rejects unwelcome elements, allowing you to reject the negative effects of poisons and diseases or shrug off other lasting negative effects. Choose one.

**Disease or Poison** Attempt a saving throw against the affliction. On a failure or critical failure, the affliction’s stage doesn’t increase.

**Persistent Damage** Attempt a DC 10 flat check to recover from the persistent damage
name: Resilient Physiology

You tap into your planar heritage and momentarily grow larger through force of will. Increase your size to Large until the beginning of your next turn. Your equipment grows with you but returns to its original size if removed. You become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1. Your reach increases by 5 feet (or by 10 feet if you started out Tiny), and you gain a +2 status bonus to melee damage. Towering Presence has no effect if you were already Large or larger.

You can cast each of those two spells once during the following day (or one spell twice if you rolled the same number) as 2nd-rank divine innate spells.

You can perform minor mischief with your tail. You can use your tail to Steal an object, though your tail still can’t hold onto it at the end of your action. If you have a hand free, you can transfer the stolen object to your hand. Otherwise, your tail surreptitiously drops it on the ground in your space, where you can grab it later. Observers other than your target who haven’t witnessed you stealing something with your tail are usually distracted, meaning the GM will usually decrease their Perception DCs, as normal for Stealing something.
name: Larcenous Tail

[Aeonbound](/Feats.aspx?ID=7195)

</column>

---

You can reorder the world with your magic. Choose two of the following spells: [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_lock_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1589), [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597), [_shape wood_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1668), or [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723). You can use each of the chosen spells once per day as 2nd-rank divine innate spells.
name: Methodical Magic

    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Impose Order](/Feats.aspx?ID=7216) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
<trait label="Nephilim" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=518" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 55

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Aeonbound](/Feats.aspx?ID=7195)

**Trigger**
You would critically fail a [skill](/Traits.aspx?ID=695) check or suffer an effect with the [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) trait.

</column>

---

You shift the underlying fabric of reality to impose a baseline of order. If the trigger was a skill check, you instead receive a result of 10 + your proficiency bonus (don’t apply any other bonuses, penalties, or modifiers). If the trigger was an effect with the misfortune trait, the misfortune and fortune effects cancel each other out as normal, negating the triggering misfortune effect.
name: Impose Order

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Nephilim" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=518" />
</traits>

once per day

</column>

---

You tap into the collective consciousness of the Godmind, the governing entity of axiomite society, to rapidly parse near-infinite variables to read your surroundings. For 1 minute, you gain precise motion sense out to 60 feet, you can see invisible creatures and objects, and you gain the benefits of [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) (with a counteract check modifier equal to your Perception modifier).
name: Channel the Godmind

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pandemonium Eruption](/Feats.aspx?ID=7218) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Nephilim" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=518" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 55

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Proteankin](/Feats.aspx?ID=7198)

</column>

---

You unleash the pent-up chaos within you, becoming a nexus of unbridled destruction that wreaks unfettered havoc on everyone in your vicinity. Each creature within 15 feet of you takes 14d6 acid, electricity, or sonic damage, chosen randomly for each target. Each target receives a basic Reflex save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC, but any creature that is subjected to the type of damage for which you currently have resistance through your Proteankin lineage takes a –2 penalty to the save. Any target that critically fails the save is also confused until the end of their next turn.
name: Pandemonium Eruption

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Animist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7219) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
<trait label="Multiclass" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=659" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 56

**Archetype** [Animist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=280)

**Prerequisites**
Wisdom +2

</column>

---

You have formed a bond with an apparition and can cast [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spells. Choose a single [apparition](Apparitions.aspx) from those available to the animist. You become bound to that apparition and can attune to it each day during your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575) to become trained in its apparition skills. If you are 8th level, you are expert in its apparition skills while attuned to it, and if you are 16th level, you are a master in its apparition skills while attuned to it. You can spend 1 day of downtime ending your relationship with this apparition and bonding to a new one. 

You gain the [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) activity. You can prepare two common cantrips each day from the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell list or any other divine cantrips you have access to, including the cantrip listed in your apparition's apparition spells. You're trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for animist archetype spells is Wisdom, and they are divine animist spells.

<title level="2">Animist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Animist Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=7220), [Spiritual Awakening](/Feats.aspx?ID=7221)
name: Animist Dedication

---

A spark of inherent divine power has lit itself within you. You become trained in martial weapons. You gain one ikon from those listed on [here](Ikons.aspx), the ability to use the ikon’s immanence and transcendence actions and effects, and the Shift Immanence action. Because you have only a single ikon, when you Spark Transcendence, your divine spark recedes back to the depths of your soul and must be recalled with Shift Immanence to re-empower your ikon. You become trained in exemplar class DC.

<title level="2">Exemplar Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Glory](/Feats.aspx?ID=7227), [Exemplar Expertise](/Feats.aspx?ID=7230), [Exemplar Resilency](/Feats.aspx?ID=7228), [Second Ikon](/Feats.aspx?ID=7231)

    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Avenger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7232) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />

</column>

---

Your training regimen has given you particular advantages when it comes to enforcing the interests of your faith. You gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spells and effects that deal [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage. You can use [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394), [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391), or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) as long as you are in a town or city with a church dedicated to your deity, extracting clues from the faithful with displays of your piety.

<title level="2">Avenger Dedication leads to...</title>
[Shadow of Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=7235), [Silence the Profane (Avenger)](/Feats.aspx?ID=7234), [Slay](/Feats.aspx?ID=7236), [Zealous Inevitability](/Feats.aspx?ID=7233)

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Zealous Inevitability](/Feats.aspx?ID=7233) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Avenger](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=282)

**Prerequisites**
[Avenger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7232)

**Requirements**
You are wielding your deity’s [favored weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=805)

</column>

---

You channel your zeal through a sacred weapon, bringing a foe closer to their demise. [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) a target with the required weapon. On a success, the target becomes [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 or increases the value of their doomed condition by 1 (most living creatures die when they’ve reached doomed 4). Creatures doomed in this way take a status penalty to their saving throws against [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spells equal to their doomed value. 

A creature that would become doomed 4 by this Strike can attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC to avoid increasing the value of their doomed condition. When this occurs, this ability gains the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait.
name: Zealous Inevitability
requirement_markdown: You are wielding your deity’s [favored weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=805)

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silence the Profane (Avenger)](/Feats.aspx?ID=7234) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 59

**Archetype** [Avenger](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=282)

**Prerequisites**
[Avenger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7232)

**Trigger**
A creature you can [observe](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) within reach of your deity’s favored weapon casts a spell.

**Requirements**
You are wielding your deity’s [favored weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=805)

</column>

---

Your training included instruction on how to prevent enemy priests from using their magic against you, a technique you have mastered and adapted. Make a Strike with your deity's favored weapon against the triggering creature. On a success, the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of your next turn. The triggering spell is disrupted on a critical success, or on a success if the target is your hunted prey and the spell is a [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spell.

**Special** If your deity’s favored weapon is a ranged weapon, this reaction can trigger if the target is within its first range increment and you can make a ranged Strike instead of a melee Strike.
name: Silence the Profane (Avenger)
requirement_markdown: You are wielding your deity’s [favored weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=805)

This is an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effect. The creature then becomes temporarily immune to your Slay for 1 day.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Haft Striker Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7237) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=601" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
<trait label="Rogue" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=687" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 59

**Requirements**
You are wielding a two-handed [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8), [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14), or [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11).

</column>

---

You enter a stance that allows you to leverage the haft of your weapon to brutal effect as an effective and frighteningly efficient weapon in its own right. You treat the haft of your wielded weapon as a simple weapon dealing 1d4 bludgeoning damage. The haft is in the [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5) group and has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) and [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602) traits. The haft shares any [fundamental runes](/Rules.aspx?ID=3167) attached to the main weapon, so long as it would normally qualify for them.

While in Haft Striker Stance, you can use feats and abilities that normally require you to be wielding two melee weapons each in a different hand, treating the haft as the second weapon, but you can’t use abilities that require you to be wielding a two-handed weapon.

<title level="2">Haft Striker Stance leads to...</title>
[Haft Beatdown](/Feats.aspx?ID=7238)
name: Haft Striker Stance
requirement_markdown: You are wielding a two-handed [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8), [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14), or [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11).

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Haft Beatdown](/Feats.aspx?ID=7238) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=601" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
<trait label="Rogue" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=687" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

once per minute

**Prerequisites**
[Haft Striker Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7237)

**Requirements**
You are in Haft Striker Stance.

</column>

---

The momentum of each of your attacks feeds into the next blow in the routine, creating a brutal rhythm that enables you to give your target one continuous beatdown. [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) twice, once with your weapon and once with its haft. Each attack counts towards your multiple attack penalty, but do not increase your penalty until you have made both attacks. Then Strike twice again, once with your weapon and once with its haft.
name: Haft Beatdown
requirement_markdown: You are in Haft Striker Stance.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bloodrager Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7239) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 60

**Archetype** [Bloodrager](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=283)

**Prerequisites**
[Bloodrager](/Instincts.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

---

Drinking powerful blood has awakened magical potential within you. You gain a spell repertoire with two cantrips of your choice, from either the [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) or [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell list. You choose these cantrips from the common spells for your tradition or from other spells you have access to on that tradition’s spell list. At least one cantrip must require a spell attack roll. You’re trained in spell attack modifier and spell DC. Your key spellcasting attribute for these spells is Charisma. Spells in your repertoire gain the [rage](/Traits.aspx?ID=680) trait while you are [raging](/Actions.aspx?ID=2802), and when you [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) from your repertoire, you become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (or increase the value of your drained condition by 1); you can reduce the value of this condition only by Harvesting Blood. 

You become trained in Arcana if you chose arcane cantrips or Religion if you chose divine cantrips. If you were already trained in this skill, you become trained in a skill of your choice. You also gain the Harvest Blood action. 

<document level="2" id="action-3065" />

<title level="2">Bloodrager Dedication leads to...</title>
[Blood Calls Blood](/Feats.aspx?ID=7240), [Hematocritical](/Feats.aspx?ID=7244), [Rising Blood Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7241), [Siphon Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7242)
name: Bloodrager Dedication

You gorge upon the magical energies of your foe’s blood. You regain one expended spell slot that must be of a lower rank than the highest rank spell you can cast. You become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 or increase the value of your drained condition by 1.

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 61 **Archetype** [Bloodrager](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=283) **Prerequisites** [Rising Blood Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7241) **Requirements** Your last action was to [Harvest Blood](/Actions.aspx?ID=3065). --- You temporarily add a 3rd-rank spell to your spell repertoire, as determined by the type of creature whose blood you just drank. If the creature has more than one of the listed traits, choose one and gain the appropriate spell. If the granted spell is not on your chosen tradition’s list, you cast the spell as though it was from your chosen tradition. The spell is removed from your spell repertoire after 24 hours, the next time you use Spelldrinker, or when you next make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), whichever comes first. ##
**Trait** **Spell** **Notes**
[Aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=523) [_vampiric feast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736)
[Animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=547) [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694)[Summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=706) creatures appear as otherworldly versions of the creature whose blood you drank.
[Celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) [_holy light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557)
[Dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=581)[_blazing bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450) Instead of [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage, this spell deals the same damage as the dragon breath of the dragon whose blood you drank (if applicable).
[Fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752)
[Fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600)[_chilling darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1464)
[Giant](/Traits.aspx?ID=616)[_pummeling rubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1642)
[Humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628)[_crisis of faith_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478)
[Monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=655)[_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929)
 **Special** If you have [Surging Blood Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7245), you can add the spell at 4th rank. If you have [Exultant Blood Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7246), you can add the spell at 7th rank.
name: Spelldrinker
requirement_markdown: Your last action was to [Harvest Blood](/Actions.aspx?ID=3065).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hematocritical](/Feats.aspx?ID=7244) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
<trait label="Rage" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=680" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Bloodrager](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=283)

**Prerequisites**
[Bloodrager Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7239)

**Requirements**
Your last action was a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) that critically hit a creature that isn’t immune to bleed damage.

</column>

---

You bathe in the arterial fluids of your enemy, drinking deep of their freed essence to empower your magic and increase your spellcasting efficacy. If your next action is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that requires a spell attack roll, you roll the spell attack roll twice and use the better result; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect. Alternatively, if your next action is to cast a spell that requires the creature damaged by the triggering strike to attempt a save, they roll twice and take the lower result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.
name: Hematocritical
requirement_markdown: Your last action was a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) that critically hit a creature that isn’t immune to bleed damage.

In addition, when you [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) from your repertoire and you are at least [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2, increase the extra damage dealt by that spell from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=2802) by an additional 2 (for a total of 3).

[Exultant Blood Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7246)

Increase the spell slots you gain from the bloodrager archetype feats by 1 for each spell rank. In addition, when you [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) from your repertoire and you are at least [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2, double the extra damage dealt by that spell from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=2802) instead.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Seneschal Witch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7247) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 62

**Archetype** [Seneschal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=284)

**Prerequisites**
Seneschal Witch

</column>

---

As you work to understand the connection with your missing patron, it’s increasingly easy to form bonds with those around you. Raw magical energy spills easily through the connection but, without a patron’s control, it sometimes resists your demands for it to form proper spells.

You gain the [Witch’s Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=5003) feat, but can’t choose your [familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=5024) or another creature with the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait as a charge. When you [cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) or [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) [_manifest will_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2147), as long as your charge is within 30 feet, you can choose to center the spell’s [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) on your charge instead of yourself.

<title level="2">Seneschal Witch Dedication leads to...</title>
[Multifaceted Will](/Feats.aspx?ID=7250), [Patron Reborn](/Feats.aspx?ID=7255), [Patron's Glamour](/Feats.aspx?ID=7248), [Patron's Whisper](/Feats.aspx?ID=7253), [Seneschal Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7249), [Spiritual Secret](/Feats.aspx?ID=7251), [Unstable Patronage](/Feats.aspx?ID=7254), [Watcher on the Wall](/Feats.aspx?ID=7252)
name: Seneschal Witch Dedication

Gathering up threads of your old connection, you can temporarily disguise yourself as your missing patron. You gain [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) as an innate spell of your patron’s tradition, automatically heightened to the rank of your witch cantrips, but you can use it only to disguise yourself as the [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) representation of your patron. Detection spells of lower rank than this _illusory disguise_ give your patron’s information rather than your own.

---

You can channel even complex spells through your connection to your charge. If your next action is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) and your charge is within 30 feet, you can have the spell originate from your charge instead. That creature can use their reaction to roughly complete the spell’s incantation, which gives the spell the [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522) trait for you (but not for them) as the manifestation appears solely around your charge.
name: Seneschal Spell

Without the firm hand of a patron, your power begins to reflect deeper complexities, sometimes latching onto entirely unexpected sources of magical potency. When you [cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) or [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) manifest will, you can choose any tradition’s manifestation. While the spell is active, you and allies in its area gain a +1 status bonus to saves against spells with that tradition trait.

You gain deeper insights into your patron’s abandoned power, allowing you to draw on that power more directly. If your next action is to [cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) a witch cantrip or witch spell that deals damage, it deals [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage instead of its normal type. It also loses any traits related to its damage, such as the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) or [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) trait, and gains the [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519) and spirit traits instead.

You claim a patron’s symbol that you can etch onto any surface. Etch a symbol onto an adjacent surface; you can see and hear what you could from the symbol’s location as long as you are on the same plane. This effect lasts until you place another symbol or the surface is [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46).

You can send a message to a creature regardless of range if they have ever been your charge. If you [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) as part of the message, the target receives a –2 circumstance penalty to their [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) DC and to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) checks to reveal the lie.

You can act as a magical patron for your charges. When you finish your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), choose a spell your [familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=5024) knows and one of your charges. Once before your next daily preparations, that creature can [Cast the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) as an innate spell; this spell can be of no higher a rank than the highest rank of spells you can cast –2.
name: Unstable Patronage

<traits>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Patron Reborn](/Feats.aspx?ID=7255) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 63

**Archetype** [Seneschal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=284)

**Prerequisites**
[Seneschal Witch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7247)

</column>

---

You have fully realized the ability to serve as a patron. 

Your newfound abilities enhance your senses, rolling back the veil of a realm hidden to most mortals where great beings vie for unimaginable influence over vast worlds. You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410), constant [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), and the ability to see into all other transitive planes that overlap the one you are in. 

You can be a patron for other witches. Choose a single type of patron you represent, such as [The Resentment](/Patrons.aspx?ID=14) or [Starless Shadow](/Patrons.aspx?ID=17). An allied witch you can observe who has that patron counts as and gains the benefits of your [Witch’s Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=5003) feat, even if you did not select them as your charge during your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575).

You learn the [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual and can impart that knowledge to your charges. Once per month, when one of your charges performs the _wish_ ritual, you can roll the skill check in their place.
name: Patron Reborn
search_markdown:
<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<additional-info>

[Vindicator](/HuntersEdge.aspx?ID=7)

</column>

---

Your church ensures that you are cared for. You can use [Religion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2976) to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394), [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391), [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), or make a [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) as long as you are in a town or city with a church dedicated to your deity. If the target of the check is also a worshipper of your deity or is your hunted prey, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the check.

<title level="2">Vindicator Dedication leads to...</title>
[Call The Hunt](/Feats.aspx?ID=7260), [Interrogate](/Feats.aspx?ID=7257), [Silence the Profane (Vindicator)](/Feats.aspx?ID=7258), [Vindicator's Judgement](/Feats.aspx?ID=7259)

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 65 **Archetype** [Vindicator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=285) **Prerequisites** [Vindicator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7256) --- You ask questions you know will be answered. Ask a question of a non-allied creature that you can see and have been conversing with. Attempt an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against the creature’s Will DC; if the creature is a member of the same religion as you, or is an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) or [werecreature](/Traits.aspx?ID=733) pretending to be a member of your faith, you get a +2 circumstance bonus on this check. The creature is then temporarily immune for 1 hour. **Critical Success** The target must directly answer your question. It doesn’t have to answer truthfully, but you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to your Perception DC if the creature attempts to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to you.
**Success** As critical success, but the circumstance bonus is +2.
**Failure** The target can refuse to answer you and becomes [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=97) if they weren’t already unfriendly or [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80).
**Critical Failure** The target refuses to answer you and becomes hostile if they weren’t already. You can’t use Interrogate on the target again for 24 hours.
name: Interrogate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silence the Profane (Vindicator)](/Feats.aspx?ID=7258) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 65

**Archetype** [Vindicator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=285)

**Prerequisites**
[Vindicator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7256)

**Trigger**
A creature you can [observe](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) within reach of your deity’s favored weapon [casts a spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734).

**Requirements**
You are wielding your deity’s [favored weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=805).

</column>

---

Your training included instruction on how to prevent enemy priests from using their magic against you, a technique you have mastered and adapted. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with your deity’s [favored weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=805) against the triggering creature. On a success, the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of your next turn. The triggering spell is disrupted on a critical success, or on a success if the target is your [hunted prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=2257) and the spell is a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell.

**Special** If your deity’s favored weapon is a ranged weapon, this reaction can trigger if the target is within its first range increment and you can make a ranged Strike instead of a melee Strike.
name: Silence the Profane (Vindicator)
requirement_markdown: You are wielding your deity’s [favored weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=805).

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Warrior Of Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7261) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 66

**Archetype** [Warrior Of Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=286)

**Prerequisites**
Warrior of legend

</column>

---

You have been given a powerful blessing of battle that is also your cursed doom. As long as you aren’t wearing heavy armor or wielding a [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12), [spears](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14) and [polearms](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11) you wield gain the [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667) trait. If the weapon already has the parry trait, increase the bonus when parrying to +2. In addition, you deal an additional amount of damage equal to your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) value with weapons in the spear and polearm groups. This damage is the same type as the required weapon.

<title level="2">Warrior Of Legend Dedication leads to...</title>
[Hero-God's Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=8017), [Heroic Defiance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7262), [Know thy Doom](/Feats.aspx?ID=7265), [Only My Doom May Claim Me](/Feats.aspx?ID=7266), [Piercing Doom](/Feats.aspx?ID=7263), [Spear of Doom](/Feats.aspx?ID=7264)
name: Warrior Of Legend Dedication

Make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306); on a success, the target takes an additional 1d10 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage and your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) value is reduced by 1.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spear of Doom](/Feats.aspx?ID=7264) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 67

**Archetype** [Warrior Of Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=286)

**Prerequisites**
[Warrior Of Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7261)

**Requirements**
An enemy hit you with an attack dealing the same damage type as your cursed weakness within the last round, and you are [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 or greater.

</column>

---

The conditions for your doom have been met, and you brace yourself for a battle that will see you either live to fight another day or die a legend. You enter the spear of doom stance. While in this stance, as long as the enemy who triggered your cursed weakness is within your reach, you can Strike them as a reaction whenever they succeed or critically succeed at an attack against you. You gain an additional reaction immediately and at the start of each of your subsequent turns that can only be used to make [Reactive Strikes](/Feats.aspx?ID=5832) against that opponent, or to make the [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) granted by this stance.

While in spear of doom stance, your cursed weakness is equal to your level instead of half your level.

<title level="2">Spear of Doom leads to...</title>
[Razor's Edge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7267)
name: Spear of Doom
requirement_markdown: An enemy hit you with an attack dealing the same damage type as your cursed weakness within the last round, and you are [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 or greater.

With sinews of bronze and thews of iron, you leap to the heavens, piercing the arrogant eyes of the gods. [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) up to your total Speed, either horizontally or vertically, and [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) twice at any point or points during the Leap. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but don’t increase your penalty until you have made both attacks.
name: Needle In The God's Eyes

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Arms that Cut the Waves](/Feats.aspx?ID=7270) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 81

</column>

---

River, lake, sea, or ocean, the water calls to you, and you don’t fear its depths. When you attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. When you would run out of air and begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439), you can instead spend a Mythic Point to hold your breath for 10 more rounds.
name: Arms that Cut the Waves

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

You excel at assisting in capturing your foes. When you attempt a check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) an opponent who’s already [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by an ally, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. On a success, you deal additional bludgeoning damage to the target equal to twice your Strength modifier.
name: Binds that Tie

</title>

You have an acute understanding of the rhythms of living things, and you never miss the quaver that betrays a spoken lie. When you attempt to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302), or when you roll Perception for [initiative](/Rules.aspx?ID=3300), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check or roll at mythic proficiency.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Feet that Stride the Sky](/Feats.aspx?ID=7273) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 81

**Prerequisites**
[Acrobat's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=1)

</column>

---

You feel the call of the sky, or perhaps a desperate urge to test the limits of the earth’s hold on you. When you or a creature you’re mounted on attempts a [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to allow you or the mount to attempt the check at mythic proficiency; determine the DC using the Long Jump DCs, and increase your maximum distance to double your Speed.
name: Feet that Stride the Sky

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hands that Unweave Disaster](/Feats.aspx?ID=7274) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 81

**Prerequisites**
[Thief's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=11)

</column>

---

Your hands move with uncanny sureness, dismantling danger. When you attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. You can expend a Mythic Point when attempting to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. As normal when attempting a check with mythic proficiency, this allows you to attempt to Disable a Device even if the check normally requires expert or greater proficiency.
name: Hands that Unweave Disaster

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Storied Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7275) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 81

**Prerequisites**
[handler's calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=7); you have an [animal companion](Companions.aspx) or [pet](/Feats.aspx?ID=5186)

**Trigger**
Your animal companion or pet would fail a saving throw.

</column>

---

Your companion is an integral part of your story, and you can expend your mythic power to protect it, defying events that would prevent it from being so. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); your companion rerolls the triggering save at mythic proficiency and gains temporary Hit Points equal to twice your level that last for 1 minute.
name: Storied Companion
prerequisite_markdown: [handler's calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=7); you have an [animal companion](Companions.aspx) or [pet](/Feats.aspx?ID=5186)
